title: Building Placement and Construction Flow
interaction_flow: 1. Player clicks (Binding.select keyTap) at world position → DesktopInput.pollInputPlayer()
2. DesktopInput: isPlacing() + mode==placing → selectX/Y = cursorX/Y, mode=placing, updateLine()
3. On Binding.select keyRelease, mode==placing + block!=null → flushPlans(linePlans) or flushPlansReverse(linePlans)
4. InputHandler.flushPlans(plans): for each BuildPlan, calls validPlace(x, y, block, rotation)
5. InputHandler.validPlace() → Build.validPlace(type, team, x, y, rotation)
6. Build.validPlaceIgnoreUnits(): checks type.isPlaceable(), env, coreRadius, block.canPlaceOn(tile, team, rot), darkness, shallows, tile occupancy
7. Build.validPlace() additionally calls checkNoUnitOverlap(type, x, y)
8. If valid: block.onNewPlan(copy) → player.unit().addBuild(copy) → playerPlanTree.insert(copy)
9. (Render loop) DesktopInput.drawUI(): block.drawPlace(cursorX, cursorY, rot, valid) → drawPotentialLinks(), drawOverlay()
10. (Render loop) InputHandler.drawBuildPlans(): block.drawPlan(plan, allPlans, valid)
11. Unit begins building: calls Build.beginPlace(unit, block, team, x, y, rotation, config) [via Call/@Remote]
12. Build.beginPlace(): re-validates via validPlace(); if block.instantBuild → directly calls ConstructBlock.constructFinish()
13. Normal path: result.beforePlaceBegan(tile, previous) → tile.setBlock(ConstructBlock.get(size), team, rotation)
14. Tile.setBlock(): sets changing=true, calls preChanged(), updates this.block, calls changeBuild(team, entityprov, rotation)
15. Tile.changeBuild(): removes old build (build.remove()), creates new Building via entityprov.get(), calls building.init(tile, team, true, rotation)
16. BuildingComp.init(): calls create(block, team) if not initialized, sets tile/rotation/position, calls add(), checkAllowUpdate(), created()
17. BuildingComp.create(block, team): sets block/team/health/maxHealth, initializes ItemModule/LiquidModule/PowerModule as needed
18. Tile.setBlock() → changed() → build.updateProximity()

19. BuildingComp.updateProximity(): clears proximity, iterates Edges.getEdges(block.size), collects adjacent buildings, calls onProximityAdded() + onProximityUpdate(), then calls onProximityUpdate() on each neighbour
20. BuildingComp.onProximityAdded(): if hasPower → updatePowerGraph()
21. (ConstructBuild update loop): builder unit contributes build progress; when progress==1 → ConstructBlock.constructed(tile, block, builder, rotation, team, config)
22. ConstructBlock.constructed(): Call.constructFinish(tile, block, builder, rotation, team, config) [@Remote server→all]
23. ConstructBlock.constructFinish(): tile.setBlock(block, team, rotation) → re-runs init/changeBuild/updateProximity chain for the REAL block
24. constructFinish: if builder==player.unit() → tile.build.playerPlaced(config); fires placeEffect, placeSound
25. ConstructBlock.constructed() (server): tile.build.placed() → if hasPower+connectedPower → PowerNode.getNodeLinks() to wire up power
26. BuildingComp.moduleBitmask() used during write/read serialization: encodes which modules (items/power/liquids/consumers) are present
27. Events.fire(BlockBuildEndEvent) broadcast to all listeners
key_method_signatures: DesktopInput: pollInputPlayer() — keyTap/keyRelease Binding.select → mode transitions
InputHandler.flushPlans(Seq<BuildPlan>): validates + enqueues plans via player.unit().addBuild()
InputHandler.validPlace(int x, int y, Block type, int rotation, BuildPlan ignore, boolean ignoreUnits): boolean
Build.validPlace(Block type, Team team, int x, int y, int rotation): boolean — delegates to validPlaceIgnoreUnits + checkNoUnitOverlap
Build.validPlaceIgnoreUnits(): checks isPlaceable(), canPlaceOn(tile,team,rot), darkness, shallows, tile occupancy loop
Block.canPlaceOn(Tile tile, Team team, int rotation): boolean — returns true by default, overridden by subclasses
Block.drawPlace(int x, int y, int rotation, boolean valid): void — drawPotentialLinks + drawOverlay
Block.drawPlan(BuildPlan plan, Eachable<BuildPlan> list, boolean valid): void
Build.beginPlace(@Nullable Unit unit, Block result, Team team, int x, int y, int rotation, Object placeConfig): @Remote(Loc.server)
Tile.setBlock(Block type, Team team, int rotation, Prov<Building> entityprov): void — preChanged, changeBuild, changed
Tile.changeBuild(Team team, Prov<Building> entityprov, int rotation): void — removes old build, creates + inits new Building
BuildingComp.init(Tile tile, Team team, boolean shouldAdd, int rotation): Building — calls create(), add(), updateProximity
BuildingComp.create(Block block, Team team): Building — wires block/team/health/modules
BuildingComp.add(): void — if hasPower, graph.checkAdd()
BuildingComp.updateProximity(): void — Edges.getEdges(size), onProximityAdded, onProximityUpdate on self + neighbours
BuildingComp.onProximityAdded(): if hasPower → updatePowerGraph()
BuildingComp.onProximityUpdate(): noSleep()
BuildingComp.placed(): @CallSuper — if hasPower → PowerNode.getNodeLinks() links power
BuildingComp.moduleBitmask(): int — bitmask of active modules (items|power|liquids|consumers)
ConstructBlock.constructFinish(Tile, Block, Unit, byte, Team, Object): @Remote(Loc.server) — sets real block, fires events
ConstructBlock.constructed(Tile, Block, Unit, byte, Team, Object): calls constructFinish + tile.build.placed()
key_relationships: InputHandler holds: BuildPlan bplan, Seq<BuildPlan> linePlans/selectPlans, QuadTree<BuildPlan> playerPlanTree
InputHandler depends_on Build (static utility for validation)
Tile owns @Nullable Building build (set via changeBuild)
Tile.changed() triggers BuildingComp.updateProximity() on the new building
BuildingComp.init() calls BuildingComp.create() for first-time setup
BuildingComp.updateProximity() calls onProximityAdded() + onProximityUpdate() on self and all adjacent Buildings
ConstructBuild (inner class of ConstructBlock) is the intermediate building during construction
branching_conditions: block.instantBuild==true → skip ConstructBuild phase, directly call constructFinish()
tile.block==result && quickRotate → just rotate, skip placement
block is Floor/OverlayFloor → setFloor/setOverlay instead of setBlock
Build.validPlace fails → plan not added to queue, shown red in drawPlan
net.client() in placed() → skip power node linking (server-only)
builder==player.unit() in constructFinish → call playerPlaced(config) for local UI feedback
data_structures: BuildPlan: {int x, y, rotation; Block block; Object config; float progress, animScale; boolean breaking, initialized, cachedValid}
BuildingComp transient fields: Tile tile, Block block, Seq<Building> proximity, int rotation
BuildingComp modules: @Nullable PowerModule power, ItemModule items, LiquidModule liquids
Tile fields: short x,y; Block block,floor,overlay; @Nullable Building build; byte data,floorData,overlayData; int extraData
important_notes: Placement is server-authoritative: Build.beginPlace and ConstructBlock.constructFinish are @Remote(Loc.server)
InputHandler queues plans client-side; unit AI processes them and calls Build.beginPlace via Call.*
DesktopInput is desktop/PC variant; MobileInput is the mobile variant — both extend InputHandler
Block.drawPlace() is a RENDER-only hook called every frame while hovering, NOT part of the placement commit
moduleBitmask() is a serialization helper (write/read), not part of the placement flow itself

updateProximity() is the mechanism by which neighbours discover the new building — it fires onProximityUpdate on all adjacent buildings, effectively updating belts/pipes/power that border the new block
PlayerComp.unit().addBuild(plan) enqueues a BuildPlan into the unit's plan queue for processing by BuilderAI
